perturbation.original_text: Regardless of the form, format, or media on or in which the Confidential Information is provided and regardless of whether any such Confidential Information is marked as such, “Confidential Information” means (1) any and all information provided by or made available by the State to the Contractor in connection with the Contract and (2) any and all personally identifiable information (PII) (including but not limited to personal information as defined in Md. Ann. Code, General Provisions §4- 101(h) and Md. Ann. Code, State Govt. § 10- 1301(c)) and protected health information (PHI) as defined in 45 CFR 160.103 that is provided by a person or entity to the Contractor in connection with this Contrac t. Confidential Information includes, by way of example only, information that the Contractor views, takes notes from, copies (if the State agrees in writing to permit copying), possesses or is otherwise provided access to and use of by the State in relati on to the Contract.
perturbation.changed_text: Regardless of the form, format, or media on or in which the Confidential Information is provided and regardless of whether any such Confidential Information is marked as such, “Confidential Information” means (1) any and all information provided by or made available by the State to the Contractor in connection with the Contract and (2) any and all personally identifiable information (PII) (including but not limited to personal information as defined in Md. Ann. Code, General Provisions §4- 101(h) and Md. Ann. Code, State Govt. § 10- 1301(c)) and protected health information (PHI) as defined in 45 CFR 160.103 that is provided by a person or entity to the Contractor in connection with this Contrac t. However, Confidential Information **excludes** publicly available information that the Contractor can obtain independently through legal means. Confidential Information includes, by way of example only, information that the Contractor views, takes notes from, copies (if the State agrees in writing to permit copying), possesses or is otherwise provided access to and use of by the State in relati on to the Contract.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exclusion for publicly available information to the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because the original definition includes 'any and all information' provided by the state, which would include information that might also be publicly available. This contradiction impacts section 2, where the Contractor is obligated to protect all Confidential Information, but now it's unclear if that includes publicly available information or not. The added words 'excludes publicly available information' causes in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Contractor shall not, without the State’s prior written consent, copy, disclose, publish, release, transfer, disseminate, use, or allow access for any purpose or in any form, any Confidential Information except for the sole and exclusive purpose of performing under the Contract. The Contractor shall limit access to the Confidential Information to the Contractor’s Personnel who have a demonstrable need to know such Confidential Information in order to perform under the Contract an d who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restr ictive as those set forth in this Agreement. Contractor shall provide copies of such agreements to the State. The names of such personnel are attached hereto and made a part hereof as Attach ment I-2. Contractor shall update Attachment I-2 by adding additional names (whether Contractor’s Personnel or a subcontractor’s personnel) as needed, from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exclusion for publicly available information to the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The original definition included 'any and all information' provided by the state, creating a contradiction as the contradicted text obligates the contractor to protect all 'Confidential Information'. Now, it's unclear if that includes publicly available information or not. This is a direct conflict impacting the scope of confidentiality obligations as defined in the contract, causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Contractor shall limit access to the Confidential Information to the Contractor’s Personnel who have a demonstrable need to know such Confidential Information in order to perform under the Contract an d who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restr ictive as those set forth in this Agreement. Contractor shall provide copies of such agreements to the State. The names of such personnel are attached hereto and made a part hereof as Attach ment I-2. Contractor shall update Attachment I-2 by adding additional names (whether Contractor’s Personnel or a subcontractor’s personnel) as needed, from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: The Contractor shall limit access to the Confidential Information to the Contractor’s Personnel who have a demonstrable need to know such Confidential Information in order to perform under the Contract an d who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restr ictive as those set forth in this Agreement. Contractor shall provide copies of such agreements to the State. The names of such personnel are attached hereto and made a part hereof as Attach ment I-2. **Contractor is not required to update Attachment I-2**
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the contractor is not required to update Attachment I-2, there is a direct contradiction with the last sentence of the original text which previously states that 'Contractor shall update Attachment I-2 by adding additional names (whether Contractor’s Personnel or a subcontractor’s personnel) as needed, from time to time.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Contractor shall not, without the State’s prior written consent, copy, disclose, publish, release, transfer, disseminate, use, or allow access for any purpose or in any form, any Confidential Information except for the sole and exclusive purpose of performing under the Contract. The Contractor shall limit access to the Confidential Information to the Contractor’s Personnel who have a demonstrable need to know such Confidential Information in order to perform under the Contract an d who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restr ictive as those set forth in this Agreement. Contractor shall provide copies of such agreements to the State. The names of such personnel are attached hereto and made a part hereof as Attach ment I-2. Contractor shall update Attachment I-2 by adding additional names (whether Contractor’s Personnel or a subcontractor’s personnel) as needed, from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states "Contractor is not required to update Attachment I-2", which directly contradicts the contradicted text's statement "Contractor shall update Attachment I-2 by adding additional names (whether Contractor’s Personnel or a subcontractor’s personnel) as needed, from time to time.". This creates a clear in-text contradiction because one clause mandates an action (updating Attachment I-2), while the other prohibits it.

perturbation.original_text: If the Contractor intends to disseminate any portion of the Confidential Information to non-employee agents who are assisting in the Contractor’s performance of the Contract or will otherwise Attac hment I – Non-Disclosure Agreement (Contractor) Page I-2 effective date: April 17, 2018 have a role in performing any aspect of the Contract, the Contra ctor shall first obtain the written consent of the State to any such dissemination. The State may grant, deny, or condition any such consent, as it may deem appropriate in its sole and absolute subjective discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Contractor intends to disseminate any portion of the Confidential Information to non-employee agents who are assisting in the Contractor’s performance of the Contract or will otherwise Attac hment I – Non-Disclosure Agreement (Contractor) Page I-2 effective date: April 17, 2018 have a role in performing any aspect of the Contract, the Contra ctor **is not required** to first obtain the written consent of the State to any such dissemination. The State may grant, deny, or condition any such consent, as it may deem appropriate in its sole and absolute subjective discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a direct contradiction. Originally, the contractor 'shall first obtain written consent' from the state. Now, it's stated the contractor 'is not required' to obtain consent. This makes it unclear whether consent is mandatory or optional, directly contradicting the initial obligation outlined in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the Contractor intends to disseminate any portion of the Confidential Information to non-employee agents who are assisting in the Contractor’s performance of the Contract or will otherwise Attac hment I – Non-Disclosure Agreement (Contractor) Page I-2 effective date: April 17, 2018 have a role in performing any aspect of the Contract, the Contra ctor shall first obtain the written consent of the State to any such dissemination. The State may grant, deny, or condition any such consent, as it may deem appropriate in its sole and absolute subjective discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states the Contractor 'shall first obtain the written consent of the State,' while the changed text states the Contractor 'is not required' to obtain consent. This creates a direct conflict, as the obligation to obtain consent is both required and not required, which fits the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Contractor hereby agrees to hol d the Confidential Information in trust and in strictest confidence, adopt or establish operating procedures and physical security measures, and take all other measures necessary to protect the Confidential Information from inadvertent release or disclosur e to unauthorized third parties and to prevent all or any portion of the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of persons not bound to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Contractor hereby agrees to hol d the Confidential Information in trust and in strictest confidence, adopt or establish operating procedures and physical security measures, and take all other measures necessary to protect the Confidential Information from inadvertent release or disclosur e to unauthorized third parties and to prevent all or any portion of the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of persons not bound to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information. **However, Contractor is not responsible for protecting Confidential Information that enters the public domain through no fault of their own.**
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'However, Contractor is not responsible for protecting Confidential Information that enters the public domain through no fault of their own' directly contradicts the initial statement, which requires the contractor to 'prevent all or any portion of the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain'. This create uncertainty over the scope of the contractor's responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Contractor hereby agrees to hol d the Confidential Information in trust and in strictest confidence, adopt or establish operating procedures and physical security measures, and take all other measures necessary to protect the Confidential Information from inadvertent release or disclosur e to unauthorized third parties and to prevent all or any portion of the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of persons not bound to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the contractor's responsibility for protecting confidential information, stating they are not responsible if it enters the public domain through no fault of their own. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, both of which require the contractor to 'prevent all or any portion of the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain'. The addition creates uncertainty and a direct conflict regarding the contractor's obligations, which aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Contractor shall, at its own expense, return to the State all Confidential Information in its care, custody, control or possession upon request of the State or on termination of the Contract.
perturbation.changed_text: The Contractor shall, at its own expense, return to the State all Confidential Information in its care, custody, control or possession upon request of the State or on termination of the Contract. **However, the Contractor is only required to return a summary of the Confidential Information, not the information itself**
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original text. The original text says the contractor returns 'all Confidential Information'. This change modifies that to require only a 'summary of the Confidential Information'. The text is internally contradictory now.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Contractor shall, at its own expense, return to the State all Confidential Information in its care, custody, control or possession upon request of the State or on termination of the Contract.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text (which are the same in this case) require the contractor to return 'all Confidential Information'. The changed text introduces a contradictory statement, specifying that the contractor is only required to return a 'summary of the Confidential Information, not the information itself.' This creates a direct conflict in the document regarding the extent of the contractor's obligation, fulfilling the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition because the obligation is defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor acknowledges that any failur e by the Contractor or the Contractor’s Personnel to abide by the terms and conditions of use of the Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm to the State and that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the State for such breach. Accor dingly, the Contractor agrees that the State may obtain an injunction to prevent the disclosure, copying or improper use of the Confidential Information. The Contractor consents to personal jurisdiction in the Maryland State Courts. The State’s rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and the State expressly reserves any and all rights, remedies, claims and actions that it may have now or in the future to protect the Confidential Information and seek damages from the Contractor and the Contractor’s Personnel for a failure to comply with the requirements of this Agreement. In the event the State suffers any losses, damages, liabilities, expenses, or costs (including, by way of example only, attorneys’ fees and disbursements) that are attributable, in whole or in part to any failure by the Contractor or any of the Contractor’s Personnel to comply with the requirements of this Agreement, the Contractor shall hold harmless and indemnify the State from and against any such losses, damages, liabilities, expenses, and costs.
perturbation.changed_text: Contractor acknowledges that any failur e by the Contractor or the Contractor’s Personnel to abide by the terms and conditions of use of the Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm to the State and that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the State for such breach. Accor dingly, the Contractor agrees that the State may obtain an injunction to prevent the disclosure, copying or improper use of the Confidential Information. The Contractor consents to personal jurisdiction in the Maryland State Courts. The State’s rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and the State expressly reserves any and all rights, remedies, claims and actions that it may have now or in the future to protect the Confidential Information and seek damages from the Contractor and the Contractor’s Personnel for a failure to comply with the requirements of this Agreement. **However, the Contractor's liability is limited to the value of the contract.** In the event the State suffers any losses, damages, liabilities, expenses, or costs (including, by way of example only, attorneys’ fees and disbursements) that are attributable, in whole or in part to any failure by the Contractor or any of the Contractor’s Personnel to comply with the requirements of this Agreement, the Contractor shall hold harmless and indemnify the State from and against any such losses, damages, liabilities, expenses, and costs.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'However, the Contractor's liability is limited to the value of the contract' directly contradicts the unlimited indemnification clause later in the paragraph. The paragraph originally states the contractor shall 'hold harmless and indemnify the State from and against any such losses, damages, liabilities, expenses, and costs', which implies unlimited liability. The new addition puts a cap on that liability, causing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor acknowledges that any failur e by the Contractor or the Contractor’s Personnel to abide by the terms and conditions of use of the Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm to the State and that monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate the State for such breach. Accor dingly, the Contractor agrees that the State may obtain an injunction to prevent the disclosure, copying or improper use of the Confidential Information. The Contractor consents to personal jurisdiction in the Maryland State Courts. The State’s rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and the State expressly reserves any and all rights, remedies, claims and actions that it may have now or in the future to protect the Confidential Information and seek damages from the Contractor and the Contractor’s Personnel for a failure to comply with the requirements of this Agreement. In the event the State suffers any losses, damages, liabilities, expenses, or costs (including, by way of example only, attorneys’ fees and disbursements) that are attributable, in whole or in part to any failure by the Contractor or any of the Contractor’s Personnel to comply with the requirements of this Agreement, the Contractor shall hold harmless and indemnify the State from and against any such losses, damages, liabilities, expenses, and costs.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'However, the Contractor's liability is limited to the value of the contract' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the unlimited indemnification clause later in the paragraph and in the Contradicted Text, which states the contractor shall 'hold harmless and indemnify the State from and against any such losses, damages, liabilities, expenses, and costs'. This implies unlimited liability in the Contradicted text (and the original text of the changed text), while the new addition explicitly caps the liability to the value of the contract, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
